user_query:
You are a senior digital workplace consultant onboarding a Fortune-500 client. You have been given a company profile. Your job is to design and build a complete, populated intranet hub — not a skeleton. A real internal workspace has DENSITY: multiple articles per channel, FAQs where they make sense, highlights, policies, leadership updates. You will use every relevant feature of the Diggspace platform. COMPANY PROFILE: --- ================================================================================ COMPANY PROFILE — APPLE INC. Source: Public information compiled for workspace onboarding purposes ================================================================================ COMPANY OVERVIEW ---------------- Legal name: Apple Inc. Founded: April 1, 1976 Headquarters: One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014, USA CEO: Tim Cook (since 2011) Employees: ~160,000 worldwide (full-time) Revenue (FY2024): $391 billion USD Fiscal year end: Last Saturday of September Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories, and sells a variety of related services. It is one of the world's most valuable companies by market capitalisation and is widely regarded as one of the most influential technology companies in history. BRAND IDENTITY -------------- Apple's brand is defined by simplicity, premium quality, and human-centred design. The visual language is clean, minimal, and typographically precise. Primary brand colors: - Apple Blue: #0071E3 (primary — CTAs, interactive elements, headers) - Pure White: #FFFFFF (primary contrast — text on blue) - Space Black: #1D1D1F (secondary — depth, dark surfaces, typography) - Signal Red: #FF3B30 (warn — alerts, warnings, product accents) - Signal Red contrast: #FFFFFF Workspace color mapping (use these when building the hub): primary_color: #0071E3 primary_color_contrast: #FFFFFF secondary_color: #1D1D1F warn_color: #FF3B30 warn_color_contrast: #FFFFFF Typography: San Francisco (proprietary), Helvetica Neue as fallback. Design philosophy: "Design is not just what it looks like. Design is how it works." — Steve Jobs Website: https://www.apple.com Careers: https://www.apple.com/careers Newsroom: https://www.apple.com/newsroom Privacy: https://www.apple.com/privacy Investor Relations: https://investor.apple.com Accessibility: https://www.apple.com/accessibility Developer: https://developer.apple.com Support: https://support.apple.com Social media: Twitter/X: https://twitter.com/Apple YouTube: https://youtube.com/Apple LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/company/apple BUSINESS DIVISIONS & PRODUCTS ------------------------------ Apple operates through five reportable segments: 1. iPhone — The world's leading smartphone. Introduced in 2007 by Steve Jobs. iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15, iPhone SE are current flagship models. Revenue contribution: ~52% of total revenue. 2. Mac — Personal computing line including MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, Mac mini, iMac, Mac Studio, and Mac Pro. Runs macOS. Transitioned to Apple Silicon (M-series) in 2020. Revenue: ~8% of total. 3. iPad — Tablet lineup: iPad Pro, iPad Air, iPad mini, iPad. Runs iPadOS. Revenue: ~4% of total. 4. Wearables, Home & Accessories — Apple Watch, AirPods, AirPods Pro, AirPods Max, HomePod, Apple TV, MagSafe accessories, Beats products. Revenue: ~10% of total. 5. Services — The fastest growing segment. Includes: - App Store (global marketplace, 36 million developers) - Apple Music (100 million songs) - Apple TV+ (original content streaming) - Apple Arcade (game subscription) - iCloud+ (storage and privacy features) - Apple Pay & Apple Card - AppleCare+ (extended warranty and support) Revenue: ~26% of total, ~$96 billion annually. OPERATIONS & RETAIL -------------------- Apple operates 516 retail stores in 25 countries. Apple Store employees are called Specialists, Geniuses (at the Genius Bar for technical support), and Creative Pros. The retail experience is a core part of Apple's brand strategy. Manufacturing is primarily handled by contract manufacturers in China (Foxconn, Pegatron), with increasing production in India and Vietnam. Supply chain is managed from Cupertino with deep supplier relationships across 43 countries. Apple publishes an annual Supplier Responsibility Report. PEOPLE & CULTURE ---------------- Apple employs ~160,000 full-time employees globally. The culture is characterised by: - Secrecy and confidentiality (employees often don't know what other teams are building) - Deep ownership mentality — small teams with high accountability - "The best or nothing" standards - Diverse and inclusive hiring practices (Apple releases an annual Diversity Report) - Benefits: comprehensive health coverage, RSU equity grants, employee product discounts, education reimbursement, free onsite health clinics, parental leave (up to 16 weeks) Employee resource groups: Black@Apple, Pride@Apple, Women@Apple, Veterans@Apple, Accessibility@Apple 2024 Glassdoor rating: 4.2/5 LinkedIn Top Companies: Consistently ranked in top 10 HR contacts: hr@apple.com Careers portal: https://www.apple.com/careers/us/ INNOVATION & R&D ----------------- Apple spends approximately $29 billion annually on R&D (FY2024). Key ongoing R&D areas: - Apple Silicon (M-series, A-series chips) — custom ARM-based processors - Vision Pro / visionOS — spatial computing platform (launched January 2024) - Health & fitness sensing (Apple Watch, research apps) - Machine learning and on-device AI (Apple Intelligence, announced 2024) - Autonomous systems (Project Titan — vehicle technology, status unclear) - Privacy-preserving technologies (differential privacy, on-device processing) Apple holds approximately 75,000 patents worldwide. CORPORATE RESPONSIBILITY & ESG -------------------------------- Environment: - Carbon neutral across corporate operations since 2020 - Goal: carbon neutral across entire supply chain and product lifecycle by 2030 - 100% renewable energy for all Apple facilities - Recycled materials in products (recycled aluminium, rare earth elements) - Apple publishes an annual Environmental Progress Report Privacy: - Core brand value: "Privacy is a fundamental human right" - App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework - On-device processing to minimise data leaving the device - Privacy labels in App Store Social impact: - $200 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative (REJI) - Apple Impact Accelerator for minority-owned businesses - ConnectED: donated technology to schools Governance: - Board of Directors: 8 members, majority independent - Chair: Arthur Levinson - Annual proxy statement published at investor.apple.com - Named to Dow Jones Sustainability Index RECENT MAJOR ANNOUNCEMENTS (2024) ----------------------------------- - Apple Intelligence: AI features integrated across iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS Sequoia. Uses on-device and Private Cloud Compute models. - Vision Pro: Spatial computing headset launched in January 2024 at $3,499. visionOS 2 announced at WWDC 2024. - iPhone 16 lineup: Announced September 2024. Camera Control button, A18 chip. - M4 chip family: M4, M4 Pro, M4 Max announced across MacBook Pro, Mac mini, iMac. - Apple Watch Series 10: Thinner design, larger display, faster charging. - Siri upgrade: Deep integration with Apple Intelligence and ChatGPT. - EU Digital Markets Act compliance: Opened App Store to alternative marketplaces in EU. - Antitrust proceedings: DOJ antitrust lawsuit filed March 2024. FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS (FY2024) ------------------------------ Revenue: $391.0B (+2% YoY) Gross margin: 46.2% Operating income: $123.2B Net income: $93.7B Cash & securities: $153.0B Debt: $101.3B EPS (diluted): $6.11 Shares buyback: $95B repurchased in FY2024 Dividend: $0.25/share per quarter Stock: AAPL — listed on NASDAQ Market cap: ~$3.4 trillion (as of late 2024) OFFICES & KEY LOCATIONS ------------------------ Apple Park (HQ): Cupertino, CA — the "spaceship" campus, opened 2017 One Infinite Loop: Cupertino, CA — original HQ, now secondary Austin, TX: Major campus, 5,000+ employees, Mac Pro manufactured here Seattle, WA: AI and machine learning hub New York, NY: Multiple offices + flagship 5th Avenue store Boston, MA: Research lab London, UK: Battersea Power Station (6,000 employees, opened 2022) Munich, Germany: European chip and wireless engineering hub Shenzhen, China: Supply chain management hub Tokyo, Japan: Regional HQ for Asia Pacific LEGAL & COMPLIANCE ------------------ Apple faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny globally: - EU Digital Markets Act — gatekeeper designation, App Store rules - DOJ antitrust (US) — smartphone monopoly allegations (filed March 2024) - Competition investigations in UK, Germany, South Korea, Japan, India - Data privacy regulators: GDPR compliance in Europe, CCPA in California Apple's legal team is one of the largest in the technology industry. Chief Legal Officer: Katherine Adams ================================================================================ END OF COMPANY PROFILE ================================================================================ AVAILABLE WORKSPACE IMAGES ─────────────────────────── These images are pre-loaded in the CMS media library. Use the exact path in the image_url field. Pick the best semantic match. Every scope and every channel should have an image — do not leave image_url null. demo/Innovation.png → Apple Intelligence, R&D, product launches, technology demo/Idea.png → strategy, ideation, creative thinking, App Store, developer demo/IdeaApplications.png → applications, product ecosystem, services, App Store demo/Startup.png → company overview, scope hero, onboarding, new employees demo/Thinking.png → leadership, executive comms, governance, analysis demo/Decision.png → executive decisions, board, investor relations, governance demo/QualityCheck.png → compliance, legal, quality assurance, regulatory demo/DataCollecting.png → analytics, research, business intelligence, supply chain demo/DataMaintenance.png → IT, infrastructure, operations, system maintenance demo/DigitalContent.png → communications, newsroom, PR, content, Apple TV+ demo/Checklist.png → HR, policy, process, AppleCare, operations demo/Winner.png → people, culture, achievements, milestones, ESG, diversity demo/Mention.png → announcements, mentions, social, Apple Newsroom demo/Message.png → internal comms, messaging, collaboration demo/Notifications.png → alerts, updates, product announcements, notifications demo/EmptyInbox.png → getting started, onboarding, inbox zero, clean slate demo/Password.png → security, privacy, IT access, credentials --- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 0 — DEEP ANALYSIS & FULL DESIGN PROPOSAL (no tool calls yet) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Read the profile carefully. Think about how this company ACTUALLY operates day-to-day — what leadership posts, what policies employees must acknowledge, what product launches are underway, what cultural rituals matter. Produce: 1. SCOPE CONFIG - Scope name (professional, matches brand voice) - image_url: pick the best match from AVAILABLE WORKSPACE IMAGES in the profile - Welcome message (1-2 sentences, specific to this company) - 5 brand hex colors (primary + contrast, secondary, warn + contrast) Use exact values from the profile's 'Workspace color mapping' block. - number_of_highlights: 4, highlights_carousel: true, highlights_style: BannerCarousel - Footer: 3 columns with real links from the profile - Social links (from profile) 2. CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5-8 channels) Design channels that reflect how THIS company communicates. No generic names. Number each channel (Ch1, Ch2, ...) — this numbering is critical for Phase 3. For each channel: - Number + Name (e.g. Ch1: Apple Newsroom) - image_url: pick from AVAILABLE WORKSPACE IMAGES — every channel needs one - Purpose, type, color, sticky, description (HTML), tabs, default_role - FAQ: 3-5 Q&A pairs for HR/Legal/Compliance/IT channels only 3. CONTENT CALENDAR Primary channels (Ch1-Ch3): 3-5 articles each. Secondary channels (Ch4+): 1-2 articles each. List articles grouped BY CHANNEL NUMBER. For each: - Channel number + name (e.g. 'Ch1: Apple Newsroom') - Title, type, tags, is_main_highlight, is_sticky, require_read_confirmation, notify, one-sentence body outline Pick 2-3 total as is_main_highlight across the whole hub. Mark require_read_confirmation=true for policies only. Article ideas — derive from the profile: Financial results, product launches, ESG commitments, ERG spotlights, compliance policies, regional office news, CEO letters, R&D milestones. 4. HUB-LEVEL POLISH company_name, website_name, default_language, supported_languages, login_quick_links (3-5 URLs), sidebar_navigation (ordered by priority). Present this full design. Then ask: 'Does this look right, or would you like to adjust anything before I build?' STOP — wait for approval before any tool call. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 1 — BUILD SCOPE ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ a. Call check_connection. b. Call create_scope with ALL design fields — no nulls: name, image_url, welcome_message, channel_creation_by_admins_only=true, all 5 theme colors, number_of_highlights=4, highlights_carousel=true, highlights_style='BannerCarousel', footer_block_links, navigation_links. c. Store the returned scope id as SCOPE_ID. d. Report: scope name / SCOPE_ID / route. Continue immediately to Phase 2. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 2 — BUILD CHANNELS ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Create every channel in design order using SCOPE_ID. Each create_channel call must include: image_url, full HTML description, channel_type, color, is_sticky, tabs, initial_tab, default_role, frequent_questions (for FAQ channels). After ALL channels are created, output this exact block — copy it verbatim into your working memory, you will reference it throughout Phase 3: CHANNEL_MAP: Ch1: [name] → [id] Ch2: [name] → [id] Ch3: [name] → [id] ... (one line per channel) Do not proceed to Phase 3 until this map is complete and every id is confirmed. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 3 — POPULATE CONTENT ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ This is the phase that makes the hub feel real. Do not shortcut it. BEFORE writing any article: print your CHANNEL_MAP. Look up the channel number from the content calendar, find its id in the map, use that id. If an id looks wrong or you are uncertain, call list_channels(SCOPE_ID) to verify. NEVER guess or construct a channel id — only use ids from CHANNEL_MAP or list_channels. Work through channels in order: finish ALL articles for Ch1, then Ch2, then Ch3, etc. After completing each channel's articles, report: 'Ch[N] [name]: [X] articles published ✓' then continue immediately to the next channel without pausing. Every article body must: - Use rich HTML:

sections,

subsections,
    /
      lists, key terms,
      for quotes, for emphasis - Be 250-500 words of REAL content — not filler - Reference specific facts from the profile (numbers, names, dates, places) - Match the company's voice (Apple: measured; finance: formal; startup: direct) - End with a CTA or reference link where appropriate Quality bar — REJECT this:

      Welcome to our company. We are committed to excellence.

      REQUIRE this:

      FY2024 Results: Revenue of $391.0B

      Apple closed its fiscal year with $391 billion in revenue, a 2 percent increase year-over-year, driven by a Services segment record of $96 billion.

      Segment performance

      • iPhone: held at 52 percent of total revenue
      • Services: all-time high, up 13% YoY
      "We have exciting plans for 2025." — Tim Cook

      Read the full results at investor.apple.com.

      After all channels are complete, verify against the design: Ch1 [name]: [X] articles (target [N]) — ✓ or ✗ Ch2 [name]: [X] articles (target [N]) — ✓ or ✗ ... If any channel is below target, write the missing articles before moving on. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 4 — HUB-WIDE POLISH ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Call update_global_settings with every field from the design: company_name, website_name, default_language, supported_languages, login_quick_links, sidebar_navigation. This completes the hub identity. Do not skip it. ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ PHASE 5 — FINAL HANDOVER ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ Deliver a clean, presentation-ready summary: [Company] Workspace — Build Complete Scope: [name] — id: [id] — /scope/[route] Brand: #[primary] / #[secondary] / warn #[warn] Channels: [count] [Ch1 name] (id: ...) — [type] — [N articles] [Ch2 name] (id: ...) — [type] — [N articles] ... Total articles published: [N] Main highlights: [list 2-3 titles] Hub language: [default] + [supported list] Close with: 'The workspace is live. Want to refine anything — adjust a channel, add regional content, or seed more articles in a specific area?' ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ ABSOLUTE RULES — breaking any of these breaks the demo ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ • Every scope and channel MUST have an image_url from the profile's image list. • NEVER guess a channel id. Only use ids from CHANNEL_MAP or list_channels. • NEVER leave description, welcome_message, or article body null or empty. • NEVER use placeholder text ('Lorem ipsum', 'TBD', 'Welcome message here'). • NEVER fabricate facts. If the profile doesn't say it, omit it. • Article bodies must use rich HTML structure — not just bare

      tags. • Every primary channel must reach its article target before Phase 4 starts. • If a tool call fails, stop — report what succeeded, ask how to proceed. • The finished workspace must feel built FOR this company, not from a template.
final_answer_preview:
PHASE 0 — DEEP ANALYSIS & FULL DESIGN PROPOSAL

Thank you for the detailed company profile. Below is a complete, design-forward proposal for a dense, high-value Apple Inc. intranet hub that leverages the full capabilities of Diggspace. I’ve grounded every element in the provided profile (numbers, places, programs, and branding) and mapped them to practical, day-to-day communications at a Fortune-5
